description:
Smell surfaced during the type-naming epic (fz-rh2.12). native.rs lowers a Prim::TypeTest by calling legacy_type_test_ty(world, ty), which re-projects a compiler2 Ty into a throwaway legacy crate::types::Ty -- a hand-written if-ladder of type_is_X probes (any, integer, float, atom, atom-singleton/union, tuple-of-arity, empty/non-empty/list, map) that spins up a fresh crate::types::new() interner per branch (13 such throwaways in native.rs) and returns None for everything else, raising "native entry-dispatch lowering does not support this type test yet". This is the same transient-interner / parallel-type-system pathology as the re-lexing the epic removes, in the backend.

Question its authority. A type-test asks "does this value belong to set T?" -- a value-membership predicate. In the set-theoretic model a type IS a set of values described by a DNF descriptor over runtime kinds (atoms, ints, floats, tuples, lists, maps, ...). compiler2 ALREADY owns that descriptor: it is the same set-theoretic kernel (see .agent/docs set-theoretic-types). So translating to a legacy Ty is a lossy, partial, per-call duplicate of structure compiler2 already has.

Goal: the runtime type-test derives its predicate directly from the compiler2 Ty value-kind projection. No legacy Ty, no throwaway interner, no fixed subset, no "not supported yet" wall -- a type-test over any compiler2 type lowers by construction because the Ty already describes exactly the set being tested.

Signal: legacy_type_test_ty and the 13 throwaway crate::types::new() in native.rs are gone; the incompleteness wall is gone; a property over arbitrary compiler2 types lowers to a correct runtime test.

Strategy / inspiration: project a Ty to a runtime value-kind predicate from its DNF axes; teach the codegen Prim::TypeTest to consume that projection. Ground it in the original semantic-subtyping work (Castagna et al.) and in ../elixir set-theoretic types -- descr.ex (the descriptor), of.ex / pattern.ex (how a descr drives guards/pattern tests). Not blocking the naming epic or the .10 gate; filed so the insight is not lost.
title: Runtime type-tests from the set-theoretic descriptor, not a legacy Ty re-projection (retire legacy_type_test_ty)
